text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Sarah Hamilton 
Consultant Neurologist  
Centre for Epilepsy  
Kingston Hospital, Galsworthy Road, KT2 7QB  

Dear Dr Sarah Hamilton, 

Alex Turner, DOB: 22-06-1991, Hospital No: K842719 NHS No. 3019928476  
Flat 12, 4 Riverside Walk, Kingston upon Thames, KT1 1AB  

Diagnosis: 

Recurrent seizures with increased frequency over recent months; prior partial response to Lamotrigine, limited benefit from Levetiracetam reported previously 

Aetiology: 

Not established at present; no structural lesion identified on prior imaging; further evaluation planned 

Other Medical Problems: 

Generalised anxiety symptoms during travel periods; vitamin D insufficiency under community management with colecalciferol 800 IU daily 

Management Plan: 

- Reinforce regular dosing of Lamotrigine; maintain single-brand supply. Current regimen: 150 mg mane and 200 mg nocte.  
- Initiate slow uptitration: increase nocte dose by 25 mg in two weeks if no adverse effects; target 150 mg mane and 225 mg nocte pending tolerability.  
- Provide a seven-day travel pill organiser and set phone reminders; pharmacy to supply two spare blister strips for carry-on luggage.  
- Safety advice reiterated, including avoidance of swimming alone, working at heights, and adherence to DVLA guidance following recent events.  
- Arrange ambulatory EEG and serum Lamotrigine level in six weeks (pre-dose trough).  
- Bloods today: FBC, U&E, LFT, vitamin D.  
- Review in eight weeks or sooner if further events. 

Clinical Summary: 

Since the last visit, this patient describes a clear worsening trend. Despite intentions to adhere, travel has become frequent and they admit to occasional missed doses, particularly evening tablets when flights are delayed. In this context, seizure activity has become more regular, with spells now occurring every one to two weeks and two events in the last month accompanied by brief loss of awareness and post-event confusion. There have been no significant injuries, but one episode resulted in a minor fall at a hotel. Sleep disruption and irregular meals during trips appear contributory. Examination today is non-focal. Prior MRI brain (2023) was unremarkable and previous EEG was inconclusive. 

I have emphasised the importance of consistent dosing and provided practical strategies tailored to travelling. We will check levels, proceed with ambulatory EEG to capture events, and cautiously adjust medication to improve control while monitoring for side effects. I have advised the patient to inform their employer regarding schedule flexibility to reduce missed doses and sleep deprivation. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: spells now occurring every one to two weeks and two events in the last month

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "spells now occurring every one to two weeks" and also mentions "two events in the last month". Convert these to allowed formats: "every one to two weeks" -> "1 per 1 to 2 week"; "two events in the last month" -> "2 per month". Applying the rule to select the highest frequency, "1 per 1 to 2 week" is equal to or more frequent than "2 per month" (range corresponds to approximately 2 to 4 per month). Therefore, the final label is "1 per 1 to 2 week".
seizure_frequency_number: spells now occurring every one to two weeks and two events in the last month

flaws.description: Gold (1 per 1 to 2 week) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
